Effective Date of 2006 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–280, title VIII, § 863(d)120 Stat. 1024Aug. 17, 2006“The amendments made by this section [enacting and amending this section] shall apply to life insurance contracts issued after the date of the enactment of this Act [], except for a contract issued after such date pursuant to an exchange described in section 1035 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 for a contract issued on or prior to that date. For purposes of the preceding sentence, any material increase in the death benefit or other material change shall cause the contract to be treated as a new contract except that, in the case of a master contract (within the meaning of section 264(f)(4)(E) of such Code), the addition of covered lives shall be treated as a new contract only with respect to such additional covered lives.”section 6039I of this titleAug. 17, 2006

L. 107–134, title I, § 102(b)115 Stat. 2429Jan. 23, 2002“(1).—Effective dateThe amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to taxable years ending before, on, or after .September 11, 2001“(2).—Waiver of limitationsIf refund or credit of any overpayment of tax resulting from the amendments made by this section is prevented at any time before the close of the 1-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [] by the operation of any law or rule of law (including res judicata), such refund or credit may nevertheless be made or allowed if claim therefor is filed before the close of such period.”Jan. 23, 2002

Effective Date of 1997 Amendment, , , as amended by ()(3)(B), , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–34, title X, § 1084(d)111 Stat. 955Pub. L. 105–206, title VI, § 6010112 Stat. 816Aug. 5, 1997July 22, 1998o“The amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 264, 265, 805, 807, 812, and 832 of this title] shall apply to contracts issued after , in taxable years ending after such date. For purposes of the preceding sentence, any material increase in the death benefit or other material change in the contract shall be treated as a new contract except that, in the case of a master contract (within the meaning of section 264(f)(4)(E) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986), the addition of covered lives shall be treated as a new contract only with respect to such additional covered lives. For purposes of this subsection, an increase in the death benefit under a policy or contract issued in connection with a lapse described in section 501(d)(2) of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 [, set out as a note under ] shall not be treated as a new contract.”June 8, 1997Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Jan. 12, 1983Oct. 22, 1986“(2)Special rule for contracts entered into before .—january 1, 1983Any contract entered into before , which meets the requirements of section 101(f) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] on the date which is 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [] shall be treated as meeting the requirements of such section for any period before the date on which such contract meets such requirements. Any death benefits paid under a flexible premium life insurance contract (within the meaning of section 101(f)(3)(A) of such Code) before the date which is 1 year after such date of enactment [] shall be excluded from gross income.January 1, 1983Sept. 3, 1982Sept. 3, 1982“(3).—Special rule for certain contractsAny contract entered into before , shall be treated as meeting the requirements of subparagraph (A) of section 101(f)(1) of such Code if such contract would meet such requirements if section 101(f)(2)(C) of such Code were applied by substituting ‘3 percent’ for ‘4 percent’.”January 1, 1983

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Jan. 12, 1983Oct. 22, 1986“(2)Special rule for contracts entered into before .—january 1, 1983Any contract entered into before , which meets the requirements of section 101(f) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] on the date which is 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [] shall be treated as meeting the requirements of such section for any period before the date on which such contract meets such requirements. Any death benefits paid under a flexible premium life insurance contract (within the meaning of section 101(f)(3)(A) of such Code) before the date which is 1 year after such date of enactment [] shall be excluded from gross income.January 1, 1983Sept. 3, 1982Sept. 3, 1982“(3).—Special rule for certain contractsAny contract entered into before , shall be treated as meeting the requirements of subparagraph (A) of section 101(f)(1) of such Code if such contract would meet such requirements if section 101(f)(2)(C) of such Code were applied by substituting ‘3 percent’ for ‘4 percent’.”January 1, 1983

children.heading: Proceeds of life insurance contracts payable by reason of death
children.label: a Proceeds of life insurance contracts payable by reason of death

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), subsection (d), subsection (f), and subsection (j), gross income does not include amounts received (whether in a single sum or otherwise) under a life insurance contract, if such amounts are paid by reason of the death of the insured.

children.label: A
children.text: if such contract or interest therein has a basis for determining gain or loss in the hands of a transferee determined in whole or in part by reference to such basis of such contract or interest therein in the hands of the transferor, or

children.label: B
children.text: if such transfer is to the insured, to a partner of the insured, to a partnership in which the insured is a partner, or to a corporation in which the insured is a shareholder or officer.

children.heading: Exception to valuable consideration rules for commercial transfers
children.label: 3 Exception to valuable consideration rules for commercial transfers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The second sentence of paragraph (2) shall not apply in the case of a transfer of a life insurance contract, or any interest therein, which is a reportable policy sale.

children.heading: Reportable policy sale
children.label: B Reportable policy sale
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “reportable policy sale” means the acquisition of an interest in a life insurance contract, directly or indirectly, if the acquirer has no substantial family, business, or financial relationship with the insured apart from the acquirer’s interest in such life insurance contract. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “indirectly” applies to the acquisition of an interest in a partnership, trust, or other entity that holds an interest in the life insurance contract.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 104–188, title I, § 1402(a)110 Stat. 1789Aug. 20, 1996
children.label: b Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 104–188, title I, § 1402(a)110 Stat. 1789Aug. 20, 1996

children.heading: Interest
children.label: c Interest
children.text: If any amount excluded from gross income by subsection (a) is held under an agreement to pay interest thereon, the interest payments shall be included in gross income.

children.heading: Payment of life insurance proceeds at a date later than death
children.label: d Payment of life insurance proceeds at a date later than death

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: The amounts held by an insurer with respect to any beneficiary shall be prorated (in accordance with such regulations as may be prescribed by the Secretary) over the period or periods with respect to which such payments are to be made. There shall be excluded from the gross income of such beneficiary in the taxable year received any amount determined by such proration. Gross income includes, to the extent not excluded by the preceding sentence, amounts received under agreements to which this subsection applies.

children.label: A
children.text: held by any insurer under an agreement provided for in the life insurance contract, whether as an option or otherwise, to pay such amount on a date or dates later than the death of the insured, and

children.label: i
children.text: as of the date of death of the insured (as if any option exercised under the life insurance contract were exercised at such time), and

children.label: ii
children.text: as discounted on the basis of the interest rate used by the insurer in calculating payments under the agreement and mortality tables prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Application of subsection
children.label: 3 Application of subsection
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any amount to which subsection (c) is applicable.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title IV, § 421(b)(2)98 Stat. 794July 18, 1984
children.label: e Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title IV, § 421(b)(2)98 Stat. 794July 18, 1984

children.heading: Proceeds of flexible premium contracts issued before payable by reason of deathJanuary 1, 1985
children.label: f Proceeds of flexible premium contracts issued before payable by reason of deathJanuary 1, 1985
i
children.text: the sum of the premiums paid under such contract does not at any time exceed the guideline premium limitation as of such time, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any amount payable by reason of the death of the insured (determined without regard to any qualified additional benefit) is not at any time less than the applicable percentage of the cash value of such contract at such time, or

children.label: B
children.text: by the terms of such contract, the cash value of such contract may not at any time exceed the net single premium with respect to the amount payable by reason of the death of the insured (determined without regard to any qualified additional benefit) at such time.

children.label: i
children.text: the guideline single premium, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the sum of the guideline level premiums to such date.

children.label: i
children.text: the mortality and other charges guaranteed under the contract, and

children.label: ii
children.text: interest at the greater of an annual effective rate of 6 percent or the minimum rate or rates guaranteed upon issue of the contract.

children.heading: Guideline level premium
children.label: C Guideline level premium
children.text: The term “guideline level premium” means the level annual amount, payable over the longest period permitted under the contract (but ending not less than 20 years from date of issue or not later than age 95, if earlier), computed on the same basis as the guideline single premium, except that subparagraph (B)(ii) shall be applied by substituting “4 percent” for “6 percent”.

children.label: i
children.text: the excess of the amount payable by reason of the death of the insured (determined without regard to any qualified additional benefit) over the cash value of the contract shall be deemed to be not greater than such excess at the time the contract was issued,

children.label: ii
children.text: the maturity date shall be the latest maturity date permitted under the contract, but not less than 20 years after the date of issue or (if earlier) age 95, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of any endowment benefit (or sum of endowment benefits) shall be deemed not to exceed the least amount payable by reason of the death of the insured (determined without regard to any qualified additional benefit) at any time under the contract.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: E Adjustments
children.text: The guideline single premium and guideline level premium shall be adjusted in the event of a change in the future benefits or any qualified additional benefit under the contract which was not reflected in any guideline single premiums or guideline level premium previously determined.

children.heading: Flexible premium life insurance contract
children.label: A Flexible premium life insurance contract
children.text: The terms “flexible premium life insurance contract” and “contract” mean a life insurance contract (including any qualified additional benefits) which provides for the payment of one or more premiums which are not fixed by the insurer as to both timing and amount. Such terms do not include that portion of any contract which is treated under State law as providing any annuity benefits other than as a settlement option.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount so returned (excluding interest) shall be deemed to reduce the sum of the premiums paid under the contract during such year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: notwithstanding the provisions of section 72(e), the amount of any interest so returned shall be includible in the gross income of the recipient.

children.label: i
children.text: 140 percent in the case of an insured with an attained age at the beginning of the contract year of 40 or less, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an insured with an attained age of more than 40 as of the beginning of the contract year, 140 percent reduced (but not below 105 percent) by one percent for each year in excess of 40.

children.heading: Cash value
children.label: D Cash value
children.text: The cash value of any contract shall be determined without regard to any deduction for any surrender charge or policy loan.

children.label: i
children.text: guaranteed insurability,

children.label: ii
children.text: accidental death benefit,

children.label: iii
children.text: family term coverage, or

children.label: iv
children.text: waiver of premium.

children.heading: Premium payments not disqualifying contract
children.label: F Premium payments not disqualifying contract
children.text: The payment of a premium which would result in the sum of the premiums paid exceeding the guideline premium limitation shall be disregarded for purposes of paragraph (1)(A)(i) if the amount of such premium does not exceed the amount necessary to prevent the termination of the contract without cash value on or before the end of the contract year.

children.label: i
children.text: the mortality basis shall be that guaranteed under the contract (determined by reference to the most recent mortality table allowed under all State laws on the date of issuance),

children.label: I
children.text: an annual effective rate of 4 percent (3 percent for contracts issued before ), orJuly 1, 1983

children.label: II
children.text: the minimum rate or rates guaranteed upon issue of the contract, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the computational rules of paragraph (2)(D) shall apply, except that the maturity date referred to in clause (ii) thereof shall not be earlier than age 95.

children.label: i
children.text: the requirements described in paragraph (1) for any contract year was not satisfied due to reasonable error, and

children.label: ii
children.text: reasonable steps are being taken to remedy the error,

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: I Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Treatment of certain accelerated death benefits
children.label: g Treatment of certain accelerated death benefits
A
children.text: Any amount received under a life insurance contract on the life of an insured who is a terminally ill individual.

children.label: B
children.text: Any amount received under a life insurance contract on the life of an insured who is a chronically ill individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If any portion of the death benefit under a life insurance contract on the life of an insured described in paragraph (1) is sold or assigned to a viatical settlement provider, the amount paid for the sale or assignment of such portion shall be treated as an amount paid under the life insurance contract by reason of the death of such insured.

children.label: I
children.text: such person is licensed for such purposes (with respect to insureds described in the same subparagraph of paragraph (1) as the insured) in the State in which the insured resides, or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an insured who resides in a State not requiring the licensing of such persons for such purposes with respect to such insured, such person meets the requirements of clause (ii) or (iii), whichever applies to such insured.

children.label: I
children.text: meets the requirements of sections 8 and 9 of the Viatical Settlements Model Act of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners, and

children.label: II
children.text: meets the requirements of the Model Regulations of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (relating to standards for evaluation of reasonable payments) in determining amounts paid by such person in connection with such purchases or assignments.

children.label: I
children.text: meets requirements similar to the requirements referred to in clause (ii)(I), and

children.label: II
children.text: meets the standards (if any) of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners for evaluating the reasonableness of amounts paid by such person in connection with such purchases or assignments with respect to chronically ill individuals.

children.heading: Special rules for chronically ill insureds
children.label: 3 Special rules for chronically ill insureds
i
children.text: such payment is for costs incurred by the payee (not compensated for by insurance or otherwise) for qualified long-term care services provided for the insured for such period, and

children.label: I
children.text: the requirements of section 7702B(b)(1)(B), and

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements (if any) applicable under subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: those requirements of section 7702B(g) and section 4980C which the Secretary specifies as applying to such a purchase, assignment, or other arrangement,

children.label: ii
children.text: standards adopted by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners which specifically apply to chronically ill individuals (and, if such standards are adopted, the analogous requirements specified under clause (i) shall cease to apply), and

children.label: iii
children.text: standards adopted by the State in which the policyholder resides (and if such standards are adopted, the analogous requirements specified under clause (i) and (subject to section 4980C(f)) standards under clause (ii), shall cease to apply).

children.heading: Per diem payments
children.label: C Per diem payments
children.text: A payment shall not fail to be described in subparagraph (A) by reason of being made on a per diem or other periodic basis without regard to the expenses incurred during the period to which the payment relates.

children.heading: Limitation on exclusion for periodic payments
children.label: D Limitation on exclusion for periodic payments
children.text: For limitation on amount of periodic payments which are treated as described in paragraph (1), see section 7702B(d).

children.heading: Terminally ill individual
children.label: A Terminally ill individual
children.text: The term “terminally ill individual” means an individual who has been certified by a physician as having an illness or physical condition which can reasonably be expected to result in death in 24 months or less after the date of the certification.

children.heading: Chronically ill individual
children.label: B Chronically ill individual
children.text: The term “chronically ill individual” has the meaning given such term by section 7702B(c)(2); except that such term shall not include a terminally ill individual.

children.heading: Qualified long-term care services
children.label: C Qualified long-term care services
children.text: The term “qualified long-term care services” has the meaning given such term by section 7702B(c).

children.heading: Physician
children.label: D Physician
children.text: The term “physician” has the meaning given to such term by section 1861(r)(1) of the Social Security Act ().42 U.S.C. 1395x(r)(1)

children.heading: Exception for business-related policies
children.label: 5 Exception for business-related policies
children.text: This subsection shall not apply in the case of any amount paid to any taxpayer other than the insured if such taxpayer has an insurable interest with respect to the life of the insured by reason of the insured being a director, officer, or employee of the taxpayer or by reason of the insured being financially interested in any trade or business carried on by the taxpayer.

children.heading: Survivor benefits attributable to service by a public safety officer who is killed in the line of duty
children.label: h Survivor benefits attributable to service by a public safety officer who is killed in the line of duty
A
children.text: if such annuity is provided, under a governmental plan which meets the requirements of section 401(a), to the spouse (or a former spouse) of the public safety officer or to a child of such officer; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent such annuity is attributable to such officer’s service as a public safety officer.

children.label: A
children.text: the death was caused by the intentional misconduct of the officer or by such officer’s intention to bring about such officer’s death;

children.label: B
children.text: the officer was voluntarily intoxicated (as defined in section 1204 of such Act) at the time of death;

children.label: C
children.text: the officer was performing such officer’s duties in a grossly negligent manner at the time of death; or

children.label: D
children.text: the payment is to an individual whose actions were a substantial contributing factor to the death of the officer.

children.heading: Certain employee death benefits payable by reason of death of certain terrorist victims or astronauts
children.label: i Certain employee death benefits payable by reason of death of certain terrorist victims or astronauts

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Gross income does not include amounts (whether in a single sum or otherwise) paid by an employer by reason of the death of an employee who is a specified terrorist victim (as defined in section 692(d)(4)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to such rules as the Secretary may prescribe, paragraph (1) shall not apply to amounts which would have been payable after death if the individual had died other than as a specified terrorist victim (as so defined).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to incidental death benefits paid from a plan described in section 401(a) and exempt from tax under section 501(a).

children.heading: Treatment of self-employed individuals
children.label: 3 Treatment of self-employed individuals
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “employee” includes a self-employed individual (as defined in section 401(c)(1)).

children.heading: Relief with respect to astronauts
children.label: 4 Relief with respect to astronauts
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall apply to any astronaut whose death occurs in the line of duty.

children.heading: Treatment of certain employer-owned life insurance contracts
children.label: j Treatment of certain employer-owned life insurance contracts

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: In the case of an employer-owned life insurance contract, the amount excluded from gross income of an applicable policyholder by reason of paragraph (1) of subsection (a) shall not exceed an amount equal to the sum of the premiums and other amounts paid by the policyholder for the contract.

children.heading: Exceptions based on insured’s status
children.label: A Exceptions based on insured’s status
i
children.text: was an employee at any time during the 12-month period before the insured’s death, or

children.label: I
children.text: a director,

children.label: II
children.text: a highly compensated employee within the meaning of section 414(q) (without regard to paragraph (1)(B)(ii) thereof), or

children.label: III
children.text: a highly compensated individual within the meaning of section 105(h)(5), except that “35 percent” shall be substituted for “25 percent” in subparagraph (C) thereof.

children.heading: Exception for amounts paid to insured’s heirs
children.label: B Exception for amounts paid to insured’s heirs
i
children.text: the amount is paid to a member of the family (within the meaning of section 267(c)(4)) of the insured, any individual who is the designated beneficiary of the insured under the contract (other than the applicable policyholder), a trust established for the benefit of any such member of the family or designated beneficiary, or the estate of the insured, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount is used to purchase an equity (or capital or profits) interest in the applicable policyholder from any person described in clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: is owned by a person engaged in a trade or business and under which such person (or a related person described in subparagraph (B)(ii)) is directly or indirectly a beneficiary under the contract, and

children.label: ii
children.text: covers the life of an insured who is an employee with respect to the trade or business of the applicable policyholder on the date the contract is issued.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “applicable policyholder” means, with respect to any employer-owned life insurance contract, the person described in subparagraph (A)(i) which owns the contract.

children.label: I
children.text: bears a relationship to the person described in clause (i) which is specified in section 267(b) or 707(b)(1), or

children.label: II
children.text: is engaged in trades or businesses with such person which are under common control (within the meaning of subsection (a) or (b) of section 52).

children.label: A
children.text: is notified in writing that the applicable policyholder intends to insure the employee’s life and the maximum face amount for which the employee could be insured at the time the contract was issued,

children.label: B
children.text: provides written consent to being insured under the contract and that such coverage may continue after the insured terminates employment, and

children.label: C
children.text: is informed in writing that an applicable policyholder will be a beneficiary of any proceeds payable upon the death of the employee.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: A Employee
children.text: The term “employee” includes an officer, director, and highly compensated employee (within the meaning of section 414(q)).

children.heading: Insured
children.label: B Insured
children.text: The term “insured” means, with respect to an employer-owned life insurance contract, an individual covered by the contract who is a United States citizen or resident. In the case of a contract covering the joint lives of 2 individuals, references to an insured include both of the individuals.
